comment: Synthetic chemo-refractory metastatic CRC, 67M, ECOG 1, left-sided sigmoid primary, RAS-mutant (KRAS G12V), BRAF-WT, MSS, post 1L FOLFOX+bev → 2L FOLFIRI+bev → PD; intent is 3L+ regorafenib (CORRECT, mOS 6.4 vs 5.0 mo HR 0.77, ReDOS dose-escalation strategy). KB DRIFT: there is currently NO ALGO-CRC-METASTATIC-3L; the engine routes (DIS-CRC, line=3) to no algorithm and emits no plan. To preserve a renderable artifact this case is encoded as line_of_therapy=2 with prior_lines reflecting both 1L and 2L exposure, so it falls through ALGO-CRC-METASTATIC-2L: step 1 (no MSI-H) → step 2 (no BRAF) → step 3 (no KRAS-G12C) → step 4 (no HER2 amp) → step 5 (RAS-mut, no rechallenge) → step 6 (RF-CRC-TRANSFORMATION-PROGRESSION does NOT fire — no cea_doubling / peritoneal / oxaliplatin-relapse trigger) → step 7 if_false default = IND-CRC-METASTATIC-3L-TAS102-BEV. The engine surfaces TAS-102+bev (SUNLIGHT) as the ENGINE-DEFAULT track; IND-CRC-METASTATIC-3L-REGORAFENIB appears as one of the alternative tracks via algo.output_indications. Drift: the brief asked for regorafenib as the headline plan, but the wired KB prefers SUNLIGHT TAS-102+bev (better tolerability + slight OS edge) and demotes regorafenib to alternative-track positioning per the indication's known_controversies + Ukraine-context access analysis. Fix would require either an ALGO-CRC-METASTATIC-3L with regorafenib as a frailty/preference branch, or a dedicated RF-CRC-TAS102-INELIGIBLE gate — both are Phase-2 KB work.
